title: eriogonum ericifolium subsp. thornei
intro: Eriogonum ericifolium subsp. thornei, also known as mountain buckwheat, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buckwheat family that is native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds. The health benefits of mountain buckwheat include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function, making it a popular herb in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions. In herbal preparations, mountain buckwheat is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and provide a range of health benefits.
